title: centaurea margaritacea subsp. margarita-alba
intro: Centaurea margaritacea subsp. margarita-alba, also known as White Cornflower, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and bloating, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea margaritacea subsp. margarita-alba include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and even have a mild sedative effect on the nervous system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies to enhance their effects.
